name: Water Dam SCADA Override
description: Compromise the SCADA system controlling a hydroelectric dam to manipulate spillway gates, reservoir levels, or turbine operations — threatening downstream flooding, infrastructure damage, or power disruption.
root_objective: Override dam SCADA controls to cause uncontrolled water release or turbine damage

nodes.title: Override Dam SCADA for Uncontrolled Water Release or Turbine Damage
nodes.description: Gain control of the dam's SCADA system to open spillway gates during high reservoir levels, disable penstock shutoff valves, or overspeed turbines — causing downstream flooding, structural damage, or generation loss.

nodes.title: Compromise Remote SCADA Access
nodes.description: Attack the remote connectivity used by operators and vendors to access the dam's SCADA system — VPN, satellite link, or cellular modem.

nodes.title: Physical Intrusion to On-Site Control Room
nodes.description: Gain physical access to the dam's control house to directly interact with SCADA HMI workstations, RTUs, or manual gate controls.

nodes.title: Exploit Vendor Remote Maintenance Access
nodes.description: Compromise the vendor's VPN or TeamViewer/AnyDesk remote access session used for periodic SCADA software maintenance on the dam's control system.

nodes.title: Attack VSAT/Cellular Communication Link
nodes.description: Intercept or hijack the VSAT satellite or cellular link connecting the remote dam to the utility control centre. Many dams use unencrypted DNP3 over satellite.

nodes.title: Command Spillway Gate Open via DNP3
nodes.description: Send DNP3 Direct Operate commands to the gate RTU to open spillway radial gates while reservoir is at capacity.

nodes.title: Bypass Perimeter Security and Access Control
nodes.description: Defeat fencing, CCTV, and badge access systems to reach the dam control house. Remote dams may have minimal on-site security personnel.

nodes.title: Access SCADA HMI and Override Controls
nodes.description: Use the on-site SCADA HMI workstation (often auto-logged-in) to switch gate control to manual mode and command gate operations.

nodes.title: Disable Safety Interlocks and Alarms
nodes.description: Prevent safety systems from triggering — disable high-water-level alarms, seismic sensors, and automatic gate closure interlocks before executing the attack.

nodes.title: SCADA HMI Auto-Login Without MFA
nodes.description: The control room HMI workstation is configured with auto-login to the SCADA software — anyone with physical access gets full operator-level control.

nodes.title: Unencrypted DNP3 over Satellite Link
nodes.description: DNP3 Secure Authentication is not implemented; SCADA commands travel unencrypted over the VSAT link and can be intercepted or injected.

nodes.title: Knowledge of Gate RTU Addressing and DNP3 Points
nodes.description: Attacker must know the DNP3 point map for the spillway gate RTU — which data points control which gates and what command sequences are valid.
